dashboards_data.name: KOI Content Extension - Alerts Dashboard
dashboards_data.description: Alert-centric dashboard shipped by the KOI Content Extension pack, over the koi_koi_raw dataset produced by the MARKETPLACE KOI pack v1.2.3 integration (13 commands, integration only). READ THIS FIRST - THE ALERTS STREAM IS DUPLICATED. The integration re-sends every still-open alert on every fetch cycle (eventFetchInterval = 1 minute), so koi_koi_raw holds one row per alert per fetch, not one row per alert. Live-verified 21 July 2026 - and note koi_koi_raw grows continuously, so every figure below is quoted against the row total it was actually measured over, not against one notional total. Over 90 days the Alerts stream carried 1,048 rows resolving to 317 distinct alerts (~3.3x); over the last 24 hours, 734 rows resolving to 3 distinct alerts (~245x). One MCP alert was re-sent 408 times over 6.8 hours, the longest re-send run observed. By cohort, in a census taken the same day over a 1,138-row window: extension alerts show a 1.0 ratio (296 rows = 296 alerts, none since 2026-07-09) and MCP alerts ~40x (842 rows = 21 alerts), and MCP is effectively all current ingestion. Every counting or aggregating widget here therefore counts DISTINCT json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id"), and every row list collapses to one row per notification with an ingested_copies column showing how many times that alert was re-sent. The dedupe key is always the INLINE json_extract_scalar, never the promoted koi_notification_id column: XSIAM applies parsing rules at ingest only and never reprocesses stored events, so any promoted column is null on every row ingested before the parsing rule shipped (on the reference tenant that is 100% of them) and a dashboard built on it would show nothing. For the same reason every other promoted column read here is coalesce(<promoted>, <guarded raw read of resources[]/observables[]>). Widgets that aggregate only with count_distinct() of an item, package or device were deliberately LEFT ALONE - count_distinct is already idempotent over duplicated rows. NO WIDGET ON THIS DASHBOARD READS AUDIT ROWS; the Audit stream is not duplicated (ratio 1.0, 20,148 rows / 20,148 records over 90 days) and would need no dedupe if one were added. COHORT WARNING: every widget that reads the item resource covers EXTENSION alerts only - 296 of 1,138 rows (26%), none since 2026-07-09 - because current ingestion is effectively 100% mcp_server and MCP alerts carry no item resource. Those widgets name the cohort in their header or title so an empty chart cannot be misread as "no risk"; they are kept because the cohort can return. MCP item version is the empty string on 831 of 842 rows while passing every null check, so the one widget that displays it guards with if(v != "", v, to_string(null)). MARKETPLACE VOCABULARY: Koi states the marketplace in two vocabularies. Audit rows and the alert observable item.marketplace use short display forms (chrome, vsc, software_windows) that the Koi API rejects with HTTP 400; the alert RESOURCE block (resources[type=item].data.marketplace), which is what every widget here reads, already uses the API long forms (chrome_web_store, vscode, windows). Every marketplace facet is named marketplace_api / mcp_registry_api and passed through the same canonicaliser as the pack parsing rule, so the label an operator reads is a value they can paste straight into a koi-* command. Values with no API equivalent are labelled rather than mixed in: built_in and side_loaded render as "not-a-marketplace: <value>", anything else unknown as "unmapped: <value>", and an empty registry as "(none)" - which is the majority case for MCP, where data.marketplace is the empty string on 705 of 716 resources. No widget depends on the modeling rule; the dashboard contains zero xdm.* references.

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| comp count_distinct(koi_notif) as total_alert
| view graph type = single subtype = standard yaxis = total_alert scale_threshold("#919291","#ff0082","50") dataunit = "Alert"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| bin _time span = 1d
| comp count() as ingested_rows, count_distinct(koi_notif) as distinct_alerts by _time
| sort asc _time
| view graph type = column subtype = grouped header = "Alert ingestion: raw rows vs distinct alerts (any gap is duplication, not volume)" xaxis = _time yaxis = ingested_rows, distinct_alerts

command.name: header
command.value: "Alert ingestion: raw rows vs distinct alerts (any gap is duplication, not volume)"

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| alter item_risk_status = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.risk_status"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.risk_status"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.risk_status"), null))
| filter item_risk_status != null
| alter assessed = if(item_risk_status = "completed", 1, 0)
| comp min(assessed) as assessed by koi_notif
| comp avg(assessed) as cov
| alter coverage_pct = multiply(cov, 100)
| view graph type = single yaxis = coverage_pct scale_threshold("#00cc88") dataunit = "%"

dataset = koi_koi_raw

| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| comp count_distinct(koi_notif) as alerts_by_severity by severity
| sort desc alerts_by_severity
| view graph type = pie subtype = full header = "Alerts by Severity" xaxis = severity yaxis = alerts_by_severity

dataset = koi_koi_raw

| sort desc alerts
| view graph type = pie header = "Alerts by Risk Level" xaxis = risk_level yaxis = alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| alter findings_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].data.findings.findings"), null))
| arrayexpand findings_arr
| alter finding_name = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.finding_name"),
        finding_severity = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.severity")
| filter finding_severity != null
| alter koi_finding_key = concat(koi_notif, "|", finding_name)
| comp count_distinct(koi_finding_key) as findings by finding_severity
| sort desc findings
| view graph type = pie subtype = donut header = "Finding Severity Mix (extension items only)" xaxis = finding_severity yaxis = findings

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| alter findings_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].data.findings.findings"), null),

        marketplace_raw = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.marketplace"), null),

| alter marketplace_api = if(

    marketplace_raw = "software_mac",     "mac",
    marketplace_raw = "chrome",           "chrome_web_store",
    marketplace_raw = "edge",             "edge_add_ons",
    marketplace_raw = "firefox",          "firefox_add_ons",
    marketplace_raw = "vsc",              "vscode",
    marketplace_raw = "jet",              "jetbrains",
    marketplace_raw = "npp",              "notepad++",
    marketplace_raw = "openvsx",          "open_vsx_registry",
    marketplace_raw = "github",           "github_mcp_registry",
    marketplace_raw in ("chocolatey", "chrome_web_store", "claude_desktop_extensions", "cursor", "docker", "edge_add_ons", "firefox_add_ons", "github_mcp_registry", "homebrew", "hugging_face", "jetbrains", "linux", "mac", "notepad++", "npm", "office_add_ins", "open_vsx_registry", "pypi", "visual_studio", "vscode", "windows", "windsurf"), marketplace_raw,
    concat("unmapped: ", marketplace_raw))
| arrayexpand findings_arr
| alter finding_name = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.finding_name")
| filter finding_name != null
| alter koi_finding_key = concat(koi_notif, "|", finding_name)
| comp count_distinct(koi_finding_key) as findings by marketplace_api, finding_name
| sort desc findings
| view graph type = column subtype = stacked header = "Finding Types by Marketplace (Koi API vocabulary; extension items only)" xaxis = marketplace_api yaxis = findings series = finding_name

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| alter marketplace_raw = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.marketplace"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.marketplace"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.marketplace"), null)),
        risk_score = to_number(coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.risk"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.risk"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.risk"), null)))
| filter marketplace_raw != null and risk_score != null
| alter marketplace_api = if(
    marketplace_raw = "", "(none)",
    marketplace_raw in ("built_in", "side_loaded"), concat("not-a-marketplace: ", marketplace_raw),
    marketplace_raw = "software_windows", "windows",
    marketplace_raw = "software_mac",     "mac",
    marketplace_raw = "chrome",           "chrome_web_store",
    marketplace_raw = "edge",             "edge_add_ons",
    marketplace_raw = "firefox",          "firefox_add_ons",
    marketplace_raw = "vsc",              "vscode",
    marketplace_raw = "jet",              "jetbrains",
    marketplace_raw = "npp",              "notepad++",
    marketplace_raw = "openvsx",          "open_vsx_registry",
    marketplace_raw = "github",           "github_mcp_registry",
    marketplace_raw in ("chocolatey", "chrome_web_store", "claude_desktop_extensions", "cursor", "docker", "edge_add_ons", "firefox_add_ons", "github_mcp_registry", "homebrew", "hugging_face", "jetbrains", "linux", "mac", "notepad++", "npm", "office_add_ins", "open_vsx_registry", "pypi", "visual_studio", "vscode", "windows", "windsurf"), marketplace_raw,
    concat("unmapped: ", marketplace_raw))
| comp min(risk_score) as risk_score by koi_notif, marketplace_api
| comp avg(risk_score) as avg_risk by marketplace_api
| sort desc avg_risk
| view graph type = column header = "Avg Risk Score by Marketplace (Koi API vocabulary; extension items only)" xaxis = marketplace_api yaxis = avg_risk

widgets_data.title: Koi Top MCP Servers
widgets_data.description: F1 DEDUPE (live-verified 2026-07-21). The Marketplace KOI pack v1.2.3 integration re-sends every still-open alert on every fetch cycle, so koi_koi_raw holds one row per alert per fetch: 1,048 alert rows over 90 days are only 317 distinct alerts, and a single MCP alert accounted for 408 of those rows. This widget counts DISTINCT json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id") INLINE - never the promoted koi_notification_id column, which is null on every row ingested before the parsing rule shipped and would leave the widget empty on historical data. Promoted parsing-rule columns are null on 100% of the 1,048 alert rows on the reference tenant (XSIAM applies parsing rules at ingest time only and never reprocesses stored events), which left this widget completely blank. Every promoted read is now coalesce(<promoted column>, <guarded raw read of resources[]/observables[]>), so the widget renders both before and after the parsing rule lands.

widgets_data.title: Koi Item Risk Level
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW resources array with a type guard (.type = "item") coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed $[0]. data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| alter mcp_registry_raw = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "mcp", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.marketplace"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "mcp", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.marketplace"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "mcp", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.marketplace"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[3].type") = "mcp", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[3].data.marketplace"), null))
| filter mcp_registry_raw != null
| alter mcp_registry_api = if(
    mcp_registry_raw = "", "(none)",
    mcp_registry_raw in ("built_in", "side_loaded"), concat("not-a-marketplace: ", mcp_registry_raw),
    mcp_registry_raw = "software_windows", "windows",
    mcp_registry_raw = "software_mac",     "mac",
    mcp_registry_raw = "chrome",           "chrome_web_store",
    mcp_registry_raw = "edge",             "edge_add_ons",
    mcp_registry_raw = "firefox",          "firefox_add_ons",
    mcp_registry_raw = "vsc",              "vscode",
    mcp_registry_raw = "jet",              "jetbrains",
    mcp_registry_raw = "npp",              "notepad++",
    mcp_registry_raw = "openvsx",          "open_vsx_registry",
    mcp_registry_raw = "github",           "github_mcp_registry",
    mcp_registry_raw in ("chocolatey", "chrome_web_store", "claude_desktop_extensions", "cursor", "docker", "edge_add_ons", "firefox_add_ons", "github_mcp_registry", "homebrew", "hugging_face", "jetbrains", "linux", "mac", "notepad++", "npm", "office_add_ins", "open_vsx_registry", "pypi", "visual_studio", "vscode", "windows", "windsurf"), mcp_registry_raw,
    concat("unmapped: ", mcp_registry_raw))
| comp count_distinct(koi_notif) as alerts by mcp_registry_api
| sort desc alerts
| view graph type = pie header = "MCP Servers by Registry (Koi API vocabulary; (none) = local or unregistered server)" xaxis = mcp_registry_api yaxis = alerts

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| alter marketplace_raw = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.marketplace"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.marketplace"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.marketplace"), null)),
        risk_score = to_number(coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.risk"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.risk"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.risk"), null)))
| filter marketplace_raw != null and risk_score != null
| alter marketplace_api = if(
    marketplace_raw = "", "(none)",
    marketplace_raw in ("built_in", "side_loaded"), concat("not-a-marketplace: ", marketplace_raw),
    marketplace_raw = "software_windows", "windows",
    marketplace_raw = "software_mac",     "mac",
    marketplace_raw = "chrome",           "chrome_web_store",
    marketplace_raw = "edge",             "edge_add_ons",
    marketplace_raw = "firefox",          "firefox_add_ons",
    marketplace_raw = "vsc",              "vscode",
    marketplace_raw = "jet",              "jetbrains",
    marketplace_raw = "npp",              "notepad++",
    marketplace_raw = "openvsx",          "open_vsx_registry",
    marketplace_raw = "github",           "github_mcp_registry",
    marketplace_raw in ("chocolatey", "chrome_web_store", "claude_desktop_extensions", "cursor", "docker", "edge_add_ons", "firefox_add_ons", "github_mcp_registry", "homebrew", "hugging_face", "jetbrains", "linux", "mac", "notepad++", "npm", "office_add_ins", "open_vsx_registry", "pypi", "visual_studio", "vscode", "windows", "windsurf"), marketplace_raw,
    concat("unmapped: ", marketplace_raw))
| comp min(risk_score) as risk_score by koi_notif, marketplace_api
| comp avg(risk_score) as avg_risk by marketplace_api
| sort desc avg_risk
| view graph type = column header = "Avg Risk Score by Marketplace (Koi API vocabulary; extension items only)" xaxis = marketplace_api yaxis = avg_risk

widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW resources array with a type guard (.type = "item") coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed $[0]. Live-verified 2026-07-21 over 90 days: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows and "mcp" on 842, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Device fields come from resources[.type = "device"] the same way. F1 DEDUPE (live-verified 2026-07-21). The Marketplace KOI pack v1.2.3 integration re-sends every still-open alert on every fetch cycle, so koi_koi_raw holds one row per alert per fetch: 1,048 alert rows over 90 days are only 317 distinct alerts, and a single MCP alert accounted for 408 of those rows. This widget counts DISTINCT json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id") INLINE - never the promoted koi_notification_id column, which is null on every row ingested before the parsing rule shipped and would leave the widget empty on historical data. This is a row list, so it dedupes by collapsing to one row per notification with | comp count() as ingested_copies by koi_notif, <displayed fields>. The ingested_copies column is deliberately kept visible: it is the number of times the integration re-sent that one alert. Before this change the list showed the same alert hundreds of times. COHORT (F5). This widget reads the item resource, which only extension alerts carry: 296 of 1,138 alert rows (26%), none since 2026-07-09, while current ingestion is effectively 100% mcp_server. The cohort is named in the header/title so an empty or shrinking chart cannot be misread as "no risk". The widget is kept, not deleted - the extension cohort can return. F4 guard: item version is emitted as the EMPTY STRING on MCP items (831 of 842 rows) while passing every null check, so item_version is wrapped in if(v != "", v, to_string(null)) - an empty version now reads as null instead of looking populated.

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| alter item_risk_status = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.risk_status"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.risk_status"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.risk_status"), null))
| filter item_risk_status != null
| alter assessed = if(item_risk_status = "completed", 1, 0)
| comp min(assessed) as assessed by koi_notif
| comp avg(assessed) as cov
| alter coverage_pct = multiply(cov, 100)
| view graph type = single yaxis = coverage_pct scale_threshold("#00cc88") dataunit = "%"

The cohort is named in the header/title so an empty or shrinking chart cannot be misread as "no risk". The widget is kept, not deleted - the extension cohort can return.

widgets_data.title: Koi Alert Ingest Duplication (rows vs distinct alerts)
widgets_data.description: THE ANTI-REGRESSION EXHIBIT for finding F1. Two series per day: ingested_rows = raw row count, distinct_alerts = count_distinct(json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")). Any gap between the bars is the Marketplace KOI integration re-sending still-open alerts on its 1-minute fetch cycle, not alert volume. Live-verified 2026-07-21 over 90 days: 1,048 rows, 317 distinct alerts (~3.3x). By cohort, in a census taken the same day over a 1,138-row window: extension alerts are 296 rows for 296 alerts (ratio 1.0) while MCP alerts are 842 rows for 21 alerts (ratio ~40, and one single alert was re-sent 408 times over 6.8 hours). If a future edit reintroduces a raw count() anywhere on this dashboard, this widget is where it shows up.

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter koi_notif = json_extract_scalar(metadata, "$.notification_event_id")
| bin _time span = 1d
| comp count() as ingested_rows, count_distinct(koi_notif) as distinct_alerts by _time
| sort asc _time
| view graph type = column subtype = grouped header = "Alert ingestion: raw rows vs distinct alerts (any gap is duplication, not volume)" xaxis = _time yaxis = ingested_rows, distinct_alerts

command.name: header
command.value: "Alert ingestion: raw rows vs distinct alerts (any gap is duplication, not volume)"
